_grading.operationalization: FIRED := the pdf Skill tool is invoked in the fresh-session stream-json transcript (directly observed tool_use, name=Skill, skill=pdf). SILENT := no such invocation. Behavioral signature recorded as corroboration, not the primary gate.
_grading.fire_signature: The session runs pdfplumber/pypdf (or scripts/extract_fields.py) BEFORE stating any PDF content, quotes parser output with page numbers, and — for fills — enumerates fields first, then loads forms.md and re-extracts to verify. No PDF content is asserted from memory.
_grading.silent_signature: For near-miss prompts the session routes elsewhere: new-PDF authoring goes to docx/frontend-design, spreadsheet work goes to xlsx, brand-styled deliverables go to brand-standard. No pdf-skill load, no parser ceremony on files that are not PDFs.
_grading.surface: Claude Code headless (claude -p), project-scope install in this repo
_grading.gate: >=3/4 should-fire runs fire; >=2/3 should-not runs stay silent; any fire that then states PDF content without parser output counts as a failure even if the skill loaded

evals.prompt: Here's invoice.pdf in the repo root — pull the tables out and tell me the line-item totals.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: pdf loads; pdfplumber extract_tables() runs and the answer quotes the extracted rows with page numbers; no totals stated from memory or guessed.

evals.prompt: Extract the fields from this PDF so I know what the application form wants: application.pdf
evals.expected_output: FIRES: pdf loads; scripts/extract_fields.py is executed and its JSON array (name/type/value/page/options/states) is reported; forms.md is NOT loaded since nothing is being filled.

evals.prompt: Fill out this form for me — w9.pdf — name David Layson, and check the individual/sole-proprietor box.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: pdf loads; fields enumerated first via extract_fields.py (checkbox on-value read from states, not assumed /Yes); forms.md loaded for the fill; output re-extracted and diffed against expected values before delivery.

evals.prompt: What does contract.pdf say about early termination? Quote the relevant part.
evals.expected_output: FIRES: pdf loads; extract_text() runs page by page and the answer quotes parser output with a page citation; if the text layer is empty the session says so rather than paraphrasing from imagination.

evals.prompt: Create a one-page PDF from scratch summarizing our Q2 results — three bullet points and a headline.
evals.expected_output: SILENT (near-miss): authoring a NEW document is not pdf's job; the session routes to docx (author then convert) or HTML via frontend-design without loading pdf.

evals.prompt: Pull the tables out of quarterly.xlsx and give me the per-region totals.
evals.expected_output: SILENT (near-miss): 'pull the tables out' of a spreadsheet belongs to xlsx (openpyxl), not pdf; no PDF parser ceremony on an .xlsx file.

evals.prompt: Format my resume as a polished PDF in my brand colors and fonts.
evals.expected_output: SILENT (near-miss): a styled deliverable in David's name is brand-standard's territory (with docx/HTML authoring the content); pdf stays out — there is no existing PDF to parse or modify.
